mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE remains below 4% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above its 12-month moving average indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of periods where INDPRO exceeds its 12mo MA for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions that drive equity market optimism, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and core inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary regime that pressures equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real sales (RSAFS) below its 6-month moving average indicates weakening consumer demand, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 50%+ of periods where RSAFS remains below its 6mo MA for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200k signals strong labor market health that supports corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening supply chain capacity, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 50%+ of periods where INDPRO remains below its 12mo MA for three consecutive months.
